revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:海人族]][[ko:해인족]]




'''Ama People''' (海人族, Kaijin-zoku or Amazoku) and '''Sea Deity Tribe''' (海神族, Watatsumi-zoku) refer to {{ill|Crowd|en|Crowd|qid=Q13430821|lt=groups|1=Crowd}} and {{ill|Clan|en|Clan|qid=Q211503|lt=clans|1=Clan}} active in maritime navigation, fishing, and oceanic endeavors during the early {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生文化|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yayoi period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弥生時代|1=Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q207858}}, gaining power in maritime transport from the 4th century onward.{{Citation needed|date=December 2024|qq=}}

They are not to be confused with the {{ill|Amabe clan|en|Amabe clan|qid=Q65272445|lt=Amabe clan}} which runs [[Kono Shrine]] or the {{ill|Amabe Caste|ja|海人部|qid=Q55528867}}

== Lineage and Distribution == 
The origins and classifications of the Ama People remain unclear due to limited research.{{Citation needed|date=December 2024|qq=}}
=== Korean Peninsula Coastal Ama Lineage ===
Many {{ill|shipwrecks|ja|船の残骸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shipwrecks|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and bronze artifacts associated with Gaya-style ships have been excavated along the southern coast of Korea, suggesting the presence of powerful pirate forces. Although there are no documented records, it is hypothesized that fishing communities in this region may have consolidated into pirate groups and migrated to the Japanese archipelago.{{Citation needed|date=December 2024|qq=}}{{Page needed|date=December 2024|qq=}}<ref name="Sawada">Sawada Yotaro, ''Mysteries of Japanese Language Formation'', Shinsensha, 1999.</ref>
=== Indonesian Lineage ===
Some hypothesize that the Ama People descended from {{ill|Austronesian peoples|en|Austronesian peoples|qid=Q861225|lt=Austronesians|1=Austronesian peoples}} who traveled to Japan via the {{ill|Kuroshio Current|en|Kuroshio Current|qid=Q53842|lt=Kuroshio Current|1=Kuroshio Current}} during the {{ill|Jōmon period|en|Jōmon period|qid=Q52824|lt=Jōmon period|1=Jōmon period}}. They are believed to have inhabited coastal areas of present-day Okinawa, Kagoshima, Miyazaki, Wakayama, Mie, Aichi, and southwestern Shizuoka prefectures.<ref name="Sawada"/>{{Page needed|date=December 2024|qq=}}
=== Indian-Chinese Lineage ===
This lineage is thought to have originated from the seafaring people of the {{ill|Minyue|en|Minyue|qid=Q1936158|lt=Minyue|1=Minyue}} region in southern China. They are believed to have migrated northward along the {{ill|East China Sea|en|East China Sea|qid=Q45341|lt=East China Sea|1=East China Sea}}, passing through the {{ill|Shandong Peninsula|en|Shandong Peninsula|qid=Q858306|lt=Shandong Peninsula|1=Shandong Peninsula}}, {{ill|Liaodong Peninsula|en|Liaodong Peninsula|qid=Q326917|lt=Liaodong Peninsula|1=Liaodong Peninsula}}, and the western coast of the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|朝鮮半島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Korean Peninsula|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Korean Peninsula|qid=Q483134}}, eventually reaching the {{ill|Genkai Sea|en|Genkai Sea|qid=Q498047|lt=Genkai Sea|1=Genkai Sea}}.<ref name="Kotobank">[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E6%B5%B7%E4%BA%BA-167836 Kotobank: World Encyclopedia Entry on Ama]</ref>
=== Yue Affiliation Theory ===
{{ill|Toshio Hōga|ja|宝賀寿男|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshio Hōga|qq=}} proposes that clans like the {{ill|Azumi people|ja|安曇氏|lt=Azumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi people|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 阿曇氏}}, {{ill|Wani|ja|和邇氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 和珥氏|1=Wani clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11417873}}, {{ill|Owari clan|en|Owari clan|qid=Q11465311|lt=Owari|1=Owari clan}}, and {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|三輪氏|lt=Miwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大神氏}} ([[Kamo clan]], [[Suwa clan]], [[Moriya clan]], [[Munakata clan]], [[Kamitsukeno clan]], [[Shimotsukeno clan]]) represent {{ill|kunitsukami|ja|地祇|lt=chthonic deities|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} lineage and originated from the coastal regions of Jiangnan, China.<ref>Toshio Hōga, ''The Lineage of the Watatsumi clan'' (Aogaki Publishing, 2012).</ref><ref>Toshio Hōga, ''The Miwa clan'' (Aogaki Publishing, 2015).</ref> Similarities between Yue languages and Japanese suggest an {{ill|Austroasiatic languages|en|Austroasiatic languages|qid=Q33199|lt=Austroasiatic languages|1=Austroasiatic languages}} influence.<ref>Yoshinori Yasumoto, ''Origins of the Japanese and Japanese Language'' (Mainichi New-{{sikyokyo|qq=}}spaper, 1991).</ref><ref>Yasumoto, ''Formation of Japanese'' (Kodansha, 1978).</ref> Genetic studies have linked Yue populations to {{ill|Haplogroup O-M268|ja|ハプログループO-M268 (Y染色体)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haplogroup O-M268|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ハプログループO1b (Y染色体)|1=Haplogroup O-M268|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q5651884}}.<ref>Mituru Sakiyama, ''A New-{{sikyokyo|qq=}} History of the Japanese Archipelago'' (Bensei Publishing, 2009).</ref> These people likely inhabited coastal regions like {{ill|Chikuzen|en|Chikuzen|qid=Q242409|lt=Chikuzen|1=Chikuzen}} and {{ill|Hizen|ja|肥前|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hizen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 肥前国|1=Hizen Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q906926}}, practicing wet rice agriculture and utilizing bronze tools.{{Citation needed|date=March 2022|qq=}}
== Clans ==
Prominent clans considered part of the Ama lineage include 
* {{ill|Azumi people|ja|安曇氏|lt=Azumi clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi people|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 阿曇氏}}
* {{ill|Munakata clan|ja|宗像氏|1=Munakata clan|fr|Clan Munakata|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata clan|qq=}}
* {{ill|Ama-no-Inukai clan|ja|海犬養氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-Inukai clan|qq=}}
* {{ill|Amabe clan|en|Amabe clan|qid=Q65272445|lt=Amabe clan|1=Amabe clan}}
* {{ill|Tsumori clan|ja|津守氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumori clan|qq=}}

== See Also ==
* {{ill|Austronesian peoples|en|Austronesian peoples|qid=Q861225|lt=Austronesian peoples|1=Austronesian peoples}}

* {{ill|Ama (diving)|en|Ama (diving)|qid=Q1577970|lt=Ama diver|1=Ama (diving)}}
* {{ill|Nakoku|en|Nakoku|qid=Q600473|lt=Nakoku|1=Nakoku}}
* {{ill|Amabe Caste|ja|海人部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama lineage|qq=}}
* {{ill|Marine navigation|en|Marine navigation|qid=Q3337280|lt=Marine navigation|1=Marine navigation}}
